verdict_reasoning: ABCB9/TAPL is a lysosomal multi-pass ABC transporter that translocates peptides from cytosol into the lysosomal lumen; its baseline residence is the lysosomal/late-endosomal membrane, not the PM. However, lysosomal TM proteins can reach the outer leaflet during lysosomal exocytosis, exposing the luminal-facing loops to the extracellular space. No documented stable PM pool, no cancer/cell-death ecto-display specifically reported, no covalent TM-partner tethering, no narrow germline-lineage surface display, no cycling secretory PM pool. Lysosomal exocytosis is the only plausible route to outer-leaflet exposure.
predicted_key_uncertainty: No gene-specific evidence that ABCB9 reaches the PM during lysosomal exocytosis; call rests on class-level lysosomal-TM analogy.
